name: Spanakopitas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, sauté green onions in butter or oil until soft.
2. Squeeze excess moisture from the thawed spinach and chop it by hand or with a food processor. Combine the chopped spinach with the remaining filling ingredients in a bowl. Mix well and set aside.
3. Lay out the Filo sheets on parchment paper, covered with waxed paper and a damp towel. Take two sheets at a time and keep the rest covered. Brush melted butter onto the top sheet of each pair.
4. Cut each double-sheet combination crosswise into equal strips, about 2-1/2 inches wide. Place about 1 teaspoon of filling 1 inch from the end nearest you on each strip.
5. Fold the strips back over the filling so the bottom edge meets the left edge, forming a right angle. Continue folding at right angles to make a triangular shape.
6. Arrange the triangles on baking sheets and cover until all are ready to bake.
7. Bake in a 350°F oven for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden and crisp, turning once. Note: Spanakopitas may be frozen before baking. When ready to bake, separate while still frozen, spread on baking sheets, and bake as above, allowing an extra 10 minutes of baking time.
